_provenance.what: Archived v1 vs v2 analyzer responses across 5 representative bench scenarios.
_provenance.why: Live LoRA serving requires a GPU; HF Space ships CPU-only. To still demonstrate the v1→v2 reward-hacking-and-fix story interactively, this file ships hand-crafted responses representing each adapter's documented behavior on these scenarios. The numbers are NOT live re-runs — they are reconstructed from documented v1 (detection=100%/FPR=36%) and v2 (logs/eval_v2.json: 99.3%/6.7%) behavior plus the per-scenario score conventions in docs/v2_error_analysis.md.
_provenance.honest_note: Treat this file as illustrative replay material, not as new measurements. The aggregate numbers (v1:100/36 vs v2:99.3/6.7) are the load-bearing claims; per-scenario scores below are representative.

scenarios.label: Scam · OTP theft · easy
scenarios.prompt: Dear customer, your SBI debit card will be blocked today due to KYC expiry. Please share the OTP sent to your number for verification.

v1.explanation: Strong urgency + bank impersonation + OTP request. SCAM.

v2.explanation: Urgency ("today") + SBI impersonation + OTP info_request — classic OTP-theft pattern.

scenarios.verdict: Both adapters catch it. Detection unchanged is the v1→v2 invariant.

scenarios.label: Benign · transaction confirmation
scenarios.prompt: Dear Customer, Rs 12,500 debited from A/c XX1234 on 15-Mar-2024 at 14:32 to VPA john@okicici. UPI Ref 12345678. If not authorised, call 1800-103-1906.

v1.explanation: Mentions debit + account number + helpline. Suspicious. SCAM.

v2.explanation: Standard transaction-debit confirmation SMS. Includes UPI Ref + helpline (legitimate format). Not a scam.

scenarios.verdict: v1 over-flags this benign (the FPR=36% fingerprint). v2 correctly recognises the legitimate transaction-debit SMS shape — this is the asymmetric improvement in action.

scenarios.label: Benign · delivery confirmation
scenarios.prompt: Your Amazon order #402-3837592-1234567 is out for delivery today. Expected between 4:00 PM and 7:00 PM. Track at amazon.in/orders.

v2.explanation: Standard Amazon delivery notification with order ID and amazon.in tracking link. Benign.

scenarios.verdict: v1 trips on "today" as urgency keyword. v2 understands legitimate delivery-window context.

scenarios.label: Scam · KYC fraud · medium
scenarios.prompt: Passport Seva: Your passport linked KYC flagged. Suspension in 48 hrs. Verify at https://passport-seva-reverify.co. Travel ban until fixed.

v1.explanation: Urgency (48h suspension) + Passport Seva impersonation + fake .co domain. SCAM.

v2.explanation: 48-hour suspension threat + Passport Seva impersonation + non-gov.in URL (passport-seva-reverify.co). KYC-fraud pattern.

scenarios.verdict: Both adapters catch. Note v2 explanation cites the specific TLD red-flag.

scenarios.label: Scam · novel deepfake CEO IPO
scenarios.prompt: [Deepfake voice of Infosys CEO]: Exclusive employee IPO allocation. Invest Rs 1 lakh via company portal. HR calling now for UPI transfer. Limited to first 100 employees.

v1.explanation: Deepfake CEO + IPO + UPI transfer ask. SCAM.

v2.explanation: Authority impersonation (CEO) + scarcity ("first 100") + investment lure routed through UPI. Post-2024 deepfake-CEO pattern — this is the kind of scenario the scripted baseline misses (caught 50% of novel; v2 catches 97%).

scenarios.verdict: Both catch. The post-2024 novel-attack class is where the scripted baseline collapses (50% detection); v2 hits 97% on novel.

delta.interpretation: The asymmetry — detection unchanged while FPR collapsed — is the signature of the model learning the task instead of gaming the reward.
